2	"""Validate Plan JSON. Standalone ŌĆö stdlib only."""
3	
4	import json
5	import re
6	import sys
7	
8	VALID_MODELS = {"", "haiku", "sonnet", "opus"}
9	VALID_EFFORTS = {"", "low", "medium", "high"}
10	VALID_TEST_LEVELS = {"", "L1", "L2"}
11	VALID_PHASE_KINDS = {"user-facing", "integration", "infrastructure"}
12	VALID_SIZES = {"standard", "large"}
13	
14	# Default caps (size="standard")
15	DEFAULT_MAX_PHASES = 4
16	DEFAULT_MAX_TASKS_PER_PHASE = 4
17	# Expanded caps (size="large") ŌĆö opt-in escape hatch for genuinely large features
18	LARGE_MAX_PHASES = 5
19	LARGE_MAX_TASKS_PER_PHASE = 5
20	
21	MAX_INFRASTRUCTURE_PHASES = 1  # independent of size ŌĆö scaffolds always monolithize
22	MAX_SETUP_TASKS = 1  # vertical slicing ŌĆö scaffold belongs in greenfield-scaffold, not PLAN.json
23	
24	# Patterns that mark a task as a horizontal-layer "setup" task. Matched against
25	# the task name (lowercased) via re.search. A task is flagged if ANY pattern
26	# matches ŌĆö we tune patterns so they catch layer-named tasks but leave real
27	# feature slices alone. A slice like "Admit an active DM-pass member" won't
28	# trip any of these; "Pydantic and dataclass models" will.
29	SETUP_TASK_PATTERNS = [
30	    # Dependencies / packages as the task's subject
31	    r"^((add|install|configure|set ?up) )?(python |node |ruby |go )?(deps|dependencies|packages)$",
32	    r"^(add|install) (python |node |ruby |go )?(deps|dependencies|packages)\b",
33	    # Models / schema as the trailing subject
34	    r"\b(pydantic|sqlalchemy|django|orm|data|dataclass|domain)( and \w+)? models?$",
35	    r"\bdata ?models?$",
36	    # Config / configuration as the subject (optionally qualified)
37	    r"^(config|configuration)( module)?( with .+)?$",
38	    # Migrations / schema as the subject
39	    r"\b(supabase |prisma |typeorm |database )?(migrations?|schema)( for (the )?(full |initial )?(data ?model|schema|tables?))?$",
40	    # Endpoints / routes / router as a TRAILING noun ("admin CRUD endpoints",
41	    # "POST /resolve endpoint", "visit query and export endpoints"). A feature
42	    # slice names the outcome, not the transport.
43	    r"\b(crud |admin |rest |api |http )?endpoints?$",
44	    r"\b(routes?|router|routing)$",
45	    # Layer-named tasks
46	    r"\b(data access|service|repository|domain|business|presentation|persistence|storage) layer$",
47	    # Event / pub-sub / message system as the subject
48	    r"\b(event|events|pub ?/? ?sub|message|messaging) (system|bus|handler|handlers|framework)$",
49	    # Standalone test-writing tasks (tests belong WITH the feature)
50	    r"\btests?$",
51	    r"\btests? for\b",
52	    # Project bootstrap
53	    r"^(project |initial )?(setup|bootstrap|scaffold(ing)?)\b",
54	]
55	
56	
57	def validate(path: str):
58	    errors = []
59	    warnings = []
60	
61	    with open(path) as f:
62	        data = json.load(f)
63	
64	    if not isinstance(data, dict):
65	        errors.append("Root must be a JSON object")
66	        return errors, warnings
67	
68	    # Required: phases
69	    if "phases" not in data:
70	        errors.append("Missing required field: 'phases'")
71	        return errors, warnings
72	
73	    phases = data["phases"]
74	    if not isinstance(phases, list):
75	        errors.append("'phases' must be a list")
76	        return errors, warnings
77	
78	    # Resolve size ŌåÆ caps
79	    size = data.get("size", "standard")
80	    if size not in VALID_SIZES:
81	        errors.append(f"'size' must be one of {sorted(VALID_SIZES)} (got '{size}')")
82	        size = "standard"  # fall through with default caps for the rest of validation
83	    max_phases = LARGE_MAX_PHASES if size == "large" else DEFAULT_MAX_PHASES
84	    max_tasks_per_phase = (
85	        LARGE_MAX_TASKS_PER_PHASE if size == "large" else DEFAULT_MAX_TASKS_PER_PHASE
86	    )
87	
88	    if len(phases) < 1:
89	        errors.append("'phases' must have at least 1 item")
90	    if len(phases) > max_phases:
91	        hint = (
92	            " Or set 'size': 'large' to raise caps to 5├Ś5 if the feature genuinely doesn't fit."
93	            if size == "standard"
94	            else ""
95	        )
96	        errors.append(
97	            f"'phases' has {len(phases)} items; max is {max_phases} (size='{size}'). "
98	            f"If the feature genuinely needs more, split the SPEC into multiple runs.{hint}"
99	        )
100	
101	    all_task_ids = {}  # task_id -> "phase[i].tasks[j]"
102	    phase_ids = {}  # phase_id -> "phases[i]"
103	    depends_on_refs = []  # (ref_task_id, location_str)
104	    phase_kinds_in_order = []  # list of (kind, phase_index, is_last_phase_flag_resolved_later)
105	    infrastructure_count = 0
106	    setup_tasks = []  # list of (location_str, task_name) flagged as setup
107	
108	    for i, phase in enumerate(phases):
109	        pprefix = f"phases[{i}]"
110	        is_last_phase = i == len(phases) - 1
111	
112	        if not isinstance(phase, dict):
113	            errors.append(f"{pprefix}: must be an object")
114	            continue
115	
116	        # phase.id
117	        if "id" not in phase:
118	            errors.append(f"{pprefix}: missing 'id'")
119	        elif not isinstance(phase["id"], str) or not phase["id"].strip():
120	            errors.append(f"{pprefix}.id: must be a non-empty string")
121	        else:
122	            pid = phase["id"]
123	            if pid in phase_ids:
124	                errors.append(
125	                    f"{pprefix}.id: duplicate phase id '{pid}' (first seen at {phase_ids[pid]})"
126	                )
127	            else:
128	                phase_ids[pid] = pprefix
129	
130	        # phase.name
131	        if "name" not in phase:
132	            errors.append(f"{pprefix}: missing 'name'")
133	        elif not isinstance(phase["name"], str) or not phase["name"].strip():
134	            errors.append(f"{pprefix}.name: must be a non-empty string")
135	
136	        # phase.kind (required)
137	        kind = phase.get("kind")
138	        if kind is None:
139	            errors.append(
140	                f"{pprefix}: missing 'kind' (one of: user-facing | integration | infrastructure)"
141	            )
142	        elif kind not in VALID_PHASE_KINDS:
143	            errors.append(f"{pprefix}.kind: '{kind}' must be one of {sorted(VALID_PHASE_KINDS)}")
144	        else:
145	            phase_kinds_in_order.append(kind)
146	            if kind == "infrastructure":
147	                infrastructure_count += 1
148	                if is_last_phase:
149	                    errors.append(
150	                        f"{pprefix}: the final phase cannot be 'infrastructure' ŌĆö "
151	                        f"every run must end on user-observable delivery"
152	                    )
153	
154	        # phase.merge_justification (required except on the last phase)
155	        mj = phase.get("merge_justification", "")
156	        if not is_last_phase:
157	            if not isinstance(mj, str) or not mj.strip():
158	                errors.append(
159	                    f"{pprefix}: missing 'merge_justification' ŌĆö "
160	                    f"one sentence on why this phase can't be merged with the next. "
161	                    f"If you can't justify it, merge them."
162	                )
163	
164	        # phase.tasks
165	        if "tasks" not in phase:
166	            errors.append(f"{pprefix}: missing 'tasks'")
167	            continue
168	
169	        tasks = phase["tasks"]
170	        if not isinstance(tasks, list):
171	            errors.append(f"{pprefix}.tasks: must be a list")
172	            continue
173	
174	        if len(tasks) < 1:
175	            errors.append(f"{pprefix}.tasks: must have at least 1 task")
176	        if len(tasks) > max_tasks_per_phase:
177	            errors.append(
178	                f"{pprefix}.tasks: has {len(tasks)} tasks; max is {max_tasks_per_phase} (size='{size}'). "
179	                f"Split into a new phase or reduce the task count."
180	            )
181	
182	        for j, task in enumerate(tasks):
183	            tprefix = f"{pprefix}.tasks[{j}]"
184	
185	            if not isinstance(task, dict):
186	                errors.append(f"{tprefix}: must be an object")
187	                continue
188	
189	            # task.id
190	            if "id" not in task:
191	                errors.append(f"{tprefix}: missing 'id'")
192	            elif not isinstance(task["id"], str) or not task["id"].strip():
193	                errors.append(f"{tprefix}.id: must be a non-empty string")
194	            else:
195	                tid = task["id"]
196	                if tid in all_task_ids:
197	                    errors.append(
198	                        f"{tprefix}.id: duplicate task id '{tid}' (first seen at {all_task_ids[tid]})"
199	                    )
200	                else:
201	                    all_task_ids[tid] = tprefix
202	
203	            # task.name
204	            if "name" not in task:
205	                errors.append(f"{tprefix}: missing 'name'")
206	            elif not isinstance(task["name"], str) or not task["name"].strip():
207	                errors.append(f"{tprefix}.name: must be a non-empty string")
208	            else:
209	                # Detect horizontal-layer "setup" tasks ŌĆö these should be folded
210	                # into the feature slices they support, not split into their own
211	                # tasks. See SKILL.md "Slice Vertically, Not Horizontally".
212	                name_lc = task["name"].strip().lower()
213	                for pattern in SETUP_TASK_PATTERNS:
214	                    if re.search(pattern, name_lc):
215	                        setup_tasks.append((tprefix, task["name"]))
216	                        break
217	
218	            # Optional: model
219	            if "model" in task:
220	                if not isinstance(task["model"], str):
221	                    errors.append(f"{tprefix}.model: must be a string")
222	                elif task["model"] not in VALID_MODELS:
223	                    errors.append(
224	                        f"{tprefix}.model: '{task['model']}' must be one of {sorted(VALID_MODELS)}"
225	                    )
226	
227	            # Optional: effort
228	            if "effort" in task:
229	                if not isinstance(task["effort"], str):
230	                    errors.append(f"{tprefix}.effort: must be a string")
231	                elif task["effort"] not in VALID_EFFORTS:
232	                    errors.append(
233	                        f"{tprefix}.effort: '{task['effort']}' must be one of {sorted(VALID_EFFORTS)}"
234	                    )
235	
236	            # Optional: test_level
237	            if "test_level" in task:
238	                if not isinstance(task["test_level"], str):
239	                    errors.append(f"{tprefix}.test_level: must be a string")
240	                elif task["test_level"] not in VALID_TEST_LEVELS:
241	                    errors.append(
242	                        f"{tprefix}.test_level: '{task['test_level']}' must be one of {sorted(VALID_TEST_LEVELS)}"
243	                    )
244	
245	            # Optional: relevant_req_ids
246	            if "relevant_req_ids" in task:
247	                rids = task["relevant_req_ids"]
248	                if not isinstance(rids, list):
249	                    errors.append(f"{tprefix}.relevant_req_ids: must be a list")
250	                else:
251	                    for rid in rids:
252	                        if not isinstance(rid, str):
253	                            errors.append(
254	                                f"{tprefix}.relevant_req_ids: each item must be a string, got {type(rid).__name__}"
255	                            )
256	                        elif not re.fullmatch(r"R\d+", rid):
257	                            errors.append(
258	                                f"{tprefix}.relevant_req_ids: '{rid}' must match pattern R\\d+"
259	                            )
260	
261	            # Optional: depends_on
262	            if "depends_on" in task:
263	                deps = task["depends_on"]
264	                if not isinstance(deps, list):
265	                    errors.append(f"{tprefix}.depends_on: must be a list")
266	                else:
267	                    for dep in deps:
268	                        if not isinstance(dep, str):
269	                            errors.append(
270	                                f"{tprefix}.depends_on: each item must be a string, got {type(dep).__name__}"
271	                            )
272	                        else:
273	                            depends_on_refs.append((dep, tprefix))
274	
275	    # Cross-phase kind rules
276	    if infrastructure_count > MAX_INFRASTRUCTURE_PHASES:
277	        errors.append(
278	            f"Plan has {infrastructure_count} infrastructure phases; max is {MAX_INFRASTRUCTURE_PHASES}. "
279	            f"Merge scaffolding/migrations/tooling into a single infrastructure phase."
280	        )
281	
282	    # Setup-task cap: vertical slicing rule. Max 1 task may be pure scaffolding
283	    # (dependencies, config, models, migrations, "REST endpoints" as a standalone
284	    # layer, etc). Beyond that, fold setup work into the first feature slice
285	    # that needs it. See SKILL.md "Slice Vertically, Not Horizontally".
286	    if len(setup_tasks) > MAX_SETUP_TASKS:
287	        flagged = ", ".join(f"{loc} ('{name}')" for loc, name in setup_tasks)
288	        errors.append(
289	            f"Plan has {len(setup_tasks)} horizontal-layer 'setup' tasks; max is "
290	            f"{MAX_SETUP_TASKS}. Flagged: {flagged}. Rewrite these as vertical "
291	            f"feature slices (e.g. 'Admit an active DM-pass member end-to-end' "
292	            f"instead of 'Add Pydantic models' + 'Create REST endpoints'). "
293	            f"Setup work belongs in the greenfield-scaffold step or folded into "
294	            f"the first feature that needs it."
295	        )
296	
297	    # Consecutive same-kind phases ŌåÆ merge candidates (warning, not error).
298	    # Skip user-facing: different user-facing phases often serve different users
299	    # (e.g. transactional staff UI vs. observability coordinator UI) ŌĆö consecutive
300	    # user-facing is typically correct, not over-slicing. Flag only integration
301	    # (real subsystem over-slicing) and infrastructure (already capped at 1; defensive).
302	    for a, b in zip(phase_kinds_in_order, phase_kinds_in_order[1:]):
303	        if a == b and a != "user-facing":
304	            warnings.append(
305	                f"Two consecutive '{a}' phases ŌĆö consider merging them unless "
306	                f"merge_justification makes a strong case for separation."
307	            )
308	            break  # one warning is enough; don't spam
309	
310	    # Validate depends_on refs point to existing task IDs
311	    for ref_id, location in depends_on_refs:
312	        if ref_id not in all_task_ids:
313	            errors.append(f"{location}.depends_on: references unknown task id '{ref_id}'")
314	
315	    return errors, warnings
316	
317	
318	def main():
319	    if len(sys.argv) != 2:
320	        print(f"Usage: {sys.argv[0]} <json-file>", file=sys.stderr)
321	        sys.exit(1)
322	    try:
323	        errors, warnings = validate(sys.argv[1])
324	    except json.JSONDecodeError as e:
325	        print(f"VALIDATION FAILED: Invalid JSON: {e}")
326	        sys.exit(1)
327	    except FileNotFoundError:
328	        print(f"VALIDATION FAILED: File not found: {sys.argv[1]}")
329	        sys.exit(1)
330	    for w in warnings:
331	        print(f"  WARNING: {w}")
332	    if errors:
333	        print(f"VALIDATION FAILED ({len(errors)} errors):")
334	        for e in errors:
335	            print(f"  ERROR: {e}")
336	        sys.exit(1)
337	    print("VALIDATION PASSED")
338	    sys.exit(0)
339	
340	
341	if __name__ == "__main__":
342	    main()
343

file.content:
---

## Step 1: Read Context

Read all available context files:

0. **Required if present:** `${DIRIGENT_RUN_DIR}/SPEC.compact.json` ŌĆö pre-compacted requirements with stable IDs (R1, R2, ŌĆ”), glossary, entities, and flows. When this exists, you MUST tag every task in the plan with its `relevant_req_ids` (see Step 3 and Rule 13).
1. **Required:** `${DIRIGENT_RUN_DIR}/SPEC.md` ŌĆö the feature specification (just written or pre-existing)
2. **Optional:** `${DIRIGENT_RUN_DIR}/BUSINESS_RULES.md` ŌĆö business rules to preserve (Legacy route)
3. **Optional:** `${DIRIGENT_RUN_DIR}/CONTEXT.md` ŌĆö relevant file analysis (Hybrid route)
4. **Optional:** `${DIRIGENT_RUN_DIR}/test-harness.json` ŌĆö e2e test harness (base URL, auth, seed data, verification commands)
5. **Optional:** `ARCHITECTURE.md` ŌĆö system architecture with XML-tagged sections. Key sections for planning:
   - `<testing-verification>` ŌĆö build/test/e2e commands, test strategy. Tasks must follow this.
   - `<architecture-decisions>` ŌĆö patterns, file organization, scaffold decisions. Tasks must follow these.
   - `<key-patterns>` ŌĆö coding conventions and patterns. Task descriptions MUST reference relevant patterns.
6. **Optional:** `.opencode/skills/` ŌĆö if this directory exists, list available skills by reading their SKILL.md frontmatter (the `name` and `description` fields). For each task, set the `convention_skills` array to skill names the coder should load. Match based on task content: Ruby files ŌåÆ `ruby-code-writing`, forms ŌåÆ `form-builder`, API endpoints ŌåÆ `api-v1-endpoints`, React ŌåÆ `react-components`, tests ŌåÆ `selenium-tests`, etc.
10. **Optional:** `.brv/context-tree/` ŌĆö if this directory exists and `brv` CLI is available, run `brv query "What are the key domain patterns, rules, and architectural decisions for this project?"` to gather domain knowledge. Incorporate relevant findings into task descriptions so coder instances have domain context.

## Step 2: Analyze the Repo

Explore the repository structure relevant to the feature. Understand:
- Project language and framework
- Existing patterns and conventions
- File organization
- Available test infrastructure

## Step 3: Create the Plan

Write `${DIRIGENT_RUN_DIR}/PLAN.json` with this exact format:

```json
{
  "title": "Feature-Titel",
  "summary": "Kurze Beschreibung was implementiert wird",
  "assumptions": ["Annahmen ueber die Codebase"],
  "out_of_scope": ["Was NICHT gemacht wird"],
  "phases": [
    {
      "id": "01",
      "name": "Phase-Name",

content.content:
237	
238	## When to use `size: "large"`
239	
240	Default is `"standard"` ŌĆö 4 phases ├Ś 4 tasks. This cap is the forcing function
241	that keeps plans atomic and reviewable. Set `"size": "large"` (raises caps to
242	5 phases ├Ś 5 tasks) only when the spec genuinely doesn't fit 4├Ś4 and the only
243	alternative is a phase densely packed with multiple `effort: "high"` tasks.
244	
245	Appropriate for `"large"`:
246	- Features with Ōēź3 distinct screens AND non-trivial logic per screen.
247	- Subsystems requiring multiple integration phases with distinct downstream contracts.
248	- Migrations that cannot be atomically split without leaving intermediate broken states.
249	
250	If you set `"large"`, the `summary` must explain why in one sentence so reviewers
251	can evaluate the expansion. Don't use `large` to avoid thinking; use it when a
252	4├Ś4 plan would compress genuinely separate work into dense phases.
253	
254	Max 1 infrastructure phase and "final phase ŌēĀ infrastructure" apply regardless
255	of size.
256	
257	## Rules
258	
259	1. **Max 4 phases ├Ś 4 tasks per phase** (default). Pydantic and the validator both reject violations. Set `"size": "large"` to raise caps to 5├Ś5 when genuinely needed ŌĆö otherwise split the SPEC into multiple dirigent runs.
260	2. **Every phase has a `kind`** ŌĆö `user-facing`, `integration`, or `infrastructure`. Required. See the table above.
261	3. **Every phase except the last has a `merge_justification`** ŌĆö one sentence, see the rule above.
262	4. **Max 1 infrastructure phase.** Scaffolds, migrations, and CI setup almost always belong together.
263	5. **Final phase MUST be `user-facing` or `integration`, not `infrastructure`.** A run that ends on infra delivered nothing observable.
264	6. **Vertical slicing ŌĆö each task delivers one user-visible outcome end-to-end.** Not a layer, not a file. See "Slice Vertically, Not Horizontally" above.
265	7. **Max 1 setup task across the whole plan** (validator-enforced). Dependencies / config / models / migrations / "REST endpoints" as standalone tasks are rejected. Fold them into the feature slice that needs them.
266	8. No dependencies between tasks within a phase.
267	9. Tasks must be concrete and executable ŌĆö each task name names a user, an action, and an outcome.
268	10. If `BUSINESS_RULES.md` exists: all rules must be preserved.
269	11. **model**: "haiku" for simple tasks, "sonnet" for standard, "opus" for complex architecture.
270	12. **effort**: "low" for mechanical, "medium" for standard, "high" for complex logic.
271	13. **test_level**: "L1" for unit tests/lint, "L2" for integration tests, empty if no testing needed.
272	14. If `test-harness.json` exists: plan verification tasks that use its verification_commands and e2e_framework.run_command.
273	15. **Plan for maintainability** ŌĆö task descriptions should guide toward scalable patterns: clear interfaces, separation of concerns, explicit dependencies.
274	16. **Plan for real verification** ŌĆö each phase will be reviewed with executable commands. The reviewer will hit real endpoints and run real test suites.
275	17. **convention_skills**: If `.opencode/skills/` exists, tag each task with the skill names the coder should load. Empty array `[]` if no convention skills are relevant.
276	18. **relevant_req_ids (spec coverage)**: If `${DIRIGENT_RUN_DIR}/SPEC.compact.json` exists, set `relevant_req_ids` on every task. Together, all tasks must collectively reference every requirement at least once. Cross-cutting requirements (encryption, audit logging, GDPR) may be referenced by multiple tasks.

file.content:
## When to use `size: "large"`

Default is `"standard"` ŌĆö 4 phases ├Ś 4 tasks. This cap is the forcing function
that keeps plans atomic and reviewable. Set `"size": "large"` (raises caps to
5 phases ├Ś 5 tasks) only when the spec genuinely doesn't fit 4├Ś4 and the only
alternative is a phase densely packed with multiple `effort: "high"` tasks.

Appropriate for `"large"`:
- Features with Ōēź3 distinct screens AND non-trivial logic per screen.
- Subsystems requiring multiple integration phases with distinct downstream contracts.
- Migrations that cannot be atomically split without leaving intermediate broken states.

If you set `"large"`, the `summary` must explain why in one sentence so reviewers
can evaluate the expansion. Don't use `large` to avoid thinking; use it when a
4├Ś4 plan would compress genuinely separate work into dense phases.

Max 1 infrastructure phase and "final phase ŌēĀ infrastructure" apply regardless
of size.

## Rules

1. **Max 4 phases ├Ś 4 tasks per phase** (default). Pydantic and the validator both reject violations. Set `"size": "large"` to raise caps to 5├Ś5 when genuinely needed ŌĆö otherwise split the SPEC into multiple dirigent runs.
2. **Every phase has a `kind`** ŌĆö `user-facing`, `integration`, or `infrastructure`. Required. See the table above.
3. **Every phase except the last has a `merge_justification`** ŌĆö one sentence, see the rule above.
4. **Max 1 infrastructure phase.** Scaffolds, migrations, and CI setup almost always belong together.
5. **Final phase MUST be `user-facing` or `integration`, not `infrastructure`.** A run that ends on infra delivered nothing observable.
6. **Vertical slicing ŌĆö each task delivers one user-visible outcome end-to-end.** Not a layer, not a file. See "Slice Vertically, Not Horizontally" above.
7. **Max 1 setup task across the whole plan** (validator-enforced). Dependencies / config / models / migrations / "REST endpoints" as standalone tasks are rejected. Fold them into the feature slice that needs them.
8. No dependencies between tasks within a phase.
9. Tasks must be concrete and executable ŌĆö each task name names a user, an action, and an outcome.
10. If `BUSINESS_RULES.md` exists: all rules must be preserved.
11. **model**: "haiku" for simple tasks, "sonnet" for standard, "opus" for complex architecture.
12. **effort**: "low" for mechanical, "medium" for standard, "high" for complex logic.
13. **test_level**: "L1" for unit tests/lint, "L2" for integration tests, empty if no testing needed.
14. If `test-harness.json` exists: plan verification tasks that use its verification_commands and e2e_framework.run_command.
15. **Plan for maintainability** ŌĆö task descriptions should guide toward scalable patterns: clear interfaces, separation of concerns, explicit dependencies.
16. **Plan for real verification** ŌĆö each phase will be reviewed with executable commands. The reviewer will hit real endpoints and run real test suites.
17. **convention_skills**: If `.opencode/skills/` exists, tag each task with the skill names the coder should load. Empty array `[]` if no convention skills are relevant.
18. **relevant_req_ids (spec coverage)**: If `${DIRIGENT_RUN_DIR}/SPEC.compact.json` exists, set `relevant_req_ids` on every task. Together, all tasks must collectively reference every requirement at least once. Cross-cutting requirements (encryption, audit logging, GDPR) may be referenced by multiple tasks.

## Validation (MANDATORY)

After writing PLAN.json, validate it:

```bash
python ${CLAUDE_SKILL_DIR}/scripts/validate_schema.py ${DIRIGENT_RUN_DIR}/PLAN.json
```

If validation fails, fix the errors and re-run until it passes.

## Implementation plan ŌĆö needs your sign-off before I touch code

**Scope:** Make QUICK route produce 1-phase ├Ś max-6-task plans. Keep AC + phase review.

### Files to change (3)

| File | Change |
|---|---|
| `src/outbid_dirigent/plan_schema.py` | Bump `Phase.tasks` `max_length` from 5 ŌåÆ 6 (current Pydantic cap blocks 6 tasks). `Plan.phases` stays at 5 (quick will only use 1). |
| `src/outbid_dirigent/plugin/skills/create-plan/scripts/validate_schema.py` | Read `${DIRIGENT_RUN_DIR}/route.json` next to the validated file. If `route == "quick"`: enforce `max_phases=1`, `max_tasks_per_phase=6`, override `size`. Reject with a clear error message if violated. |
| `src/outbid_dirigent/plugin/skills/create-plan/SKILL.md` | Add `route.json` to the inputs list. Add a "Quick route override" section: 1├Ś6, no `size: large` for quick, no `merge_justification` needed (only one phase). Update Rule 1. |

### Files NOT changed (deliberate)

- `executor.py` ŌĆö phase contract + phase review stay (your call: "Review and AC can stay")
- `router.py` ŌĆö `QUICK_STEPS` stay identical
- `dirigent.py` ŌĆö no CLI changes; `--route quick` already works

### Verification

| Check | Command |
|---|---|
| Existing schema tests pass | `pytest tests/test_plan_schema.py` |
| New validator tests pass | New `tests/test_validate_schema.py` covering: quick-1├Ś6 OK, quick-2├Ś rejected, quick-7-tasks rejected, standard 4├Ś4 still OK |
| Lint clean | `ruff check src/ tests/` |
| Pyright clean | `pyright src/` |

### Commit shape

One commit on a feature branch:
- `feat(quick-route): cap plans at 1 phase ├Ś 6 tasks`

Conventional Commits per CLAUDE.md, AI co-author trailer per the agentic-development policy.

### Two open questions before I start

1. **Pydantic task cap**: 6 is the new floor that allows quick. Standard/large stay capped at 4/5 by the validator. OK to bump Pydantic to 6?
2. **Branch + PR or just commit on master?** Project standards say "peer review required, no self-merging" ŌĆö so this needs a branch + PR. Confirm you want me to push + open a PR after committing?

"""
Plan schema ŌĆö typed representation of PLAN.json.

Uses pydantic for validation, serialization, and deserialization.
"""

import json
import logging
from enum import Enum
from pathlib import Path
from typing import Literal, Optional

from pydantic import BaseModel, Field

logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)


class PhaseKind(str, Enum):
    """How a phase relates to the user.

    Drives layer quotas in contracts and plan-level validation (max 1
    infrastructure, final phase ŌēĀ infrastructure, etc).
    """

    USER_FACING = "user-facing"  # Delivers UI surface or observable behavior change
    INTEGRATION = "integration"  # Subsystem a later phase will expose to users
    INFRASTRUCTURE = "infrastructure"  # Scaffolding, migrations, tooling ŌĆö no consumer in-run


class Task(BaseModel):
    """A single executable task within a phase."""

    id: str
    name: str
    description: str = ""
    files_to_create: list[str] = Field(default_factory=list)
    files_to_modify: list[str] = Field(default_factory=list)
    depends_on: list[str] = Field(default_factory=list)
    model: Literal["", "haiku", "sonnet", "opus"] = ""
    effort: Literal["", "low", "medium", "high", "xhigh", "max"] = ""
    test_level: Literal["", "L1", "L2"] = ""
    convention_skills: list[str] = Field(
        default_factory=list
    )  # e.g. ["ruby-code-writing", "form-builder"]
    relevant_req_ids: list[str] = Field(
        default_factory=list
    )  # e.g. ["R3", "R7"] from SPEC.compact.json


class Phase(BaseModel):
    """A group of related tasks executed sequentially."""

    id: str
    name: str
    kind: PhaseKind = Field(
        default=PhaseKind.USER_FACING,
        description="user-facing | integration | infrastructure ŌĆö drives contract layer quotas",
    )
    description: str = ""
    merge_justification: str = Field(
        default="",
        description="One sentence: why this phase can't be merged with the next. Empty only if this is the last phase.",
    )
    tasks: list[Task] = Field(default_factory=list, max_length=5)

    def model_post_init(self, __context):
        # Normalize: accept both "id" and "phase" field names
        if not self.id and hasattr(self, "phase"):
            self.id = str(getattr(self, "phase"))


class Plan(BaseModel):
    """The full execution plan for a dirigent run."""

    title: str = "Untitled"
    summary: str = ""
    size: Literal["standard", "large"] = Field(
        default="standard",
        description="'large' raises validator caps from 4 phases ├Ś 4 tasks to 5 ├Ś 5. Use only when the feature genuinely cannot fit 4├Ś4 ŌĆö summary must justify.",
    )
    phases: list[Phase] = Field(default_factory=list, max_length=5)
    estimated_complexity: str = "medium"
    risks: list[str] = Field(default_factory=list)
    assumptions: list[str] = Field(default_factory=list)
    out_of_scope: list[str] = Field(default_factory=list)

    @property
    def total_tasks(self) -> int:
        return sum(len(p.tasks) for p in self.phases)

    @property
    def all_tasks(self) -> list[tuple[Task, Phase]]:
        """Flat list of (task, phase) in execution order."""
        return [(t, p) for p in self.phases for t in p.tasks]

    def task_position(self, task_id: str) -> Optional[dict]:
        """Position info for a task: index, total, phase, prev/next."""
        tasks = self.all_tasks
        idx = next((i for i, (t, _) in enumerate(tasks) if t.id == task_id), None)
        if idx is None:
            return None

        t, p = tasks[idx]
        info = {
            "index": idx + 1,
            "total": len(tasks),
            "phase_id": p.id,
            "phase_name": p.name,
            "total_phases": len(self.phases),
        }
        if idx > 0:
            prev_t, _ = tasks[idx - 1]
            info["prev_id"] = prev_t.id
            info["prev_name"] = prev_t.name
        if idx < len(tasks) - 1:
            next_t, _ = tasks[idx + 1]
            info["next_id"] = next_t.id
            info["next_name"] = next_t.name
        return info

    def save(self, path: Path):
        path.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
        path.write_text(
            self.model_dump_json(indent=2, exclude_none=True),
            encoding="utf-8",
        )

    @classmethod
    def load(cls, path: Path) -> Optional["Plan"]:
        """Load from PLAN.json. Returns None if missing or invalid."""
        if not path.exists():
            return None
        try:
            raw = json.loads(path.read_text(encoding="utf-8"))
            # Normalize phase/task fields from common LLM output variations
            for p in raw.get("phases", []):
                if "phase" in p and "id" not in p:
                    p["id"] = str(p.pop("phase"))
                elif "id" in p:
                    p["id"] = str(p["id"])
                # Pre-plan-tuning plans have no phase kind ŌĆö default to user-facing
                # (tightest downstream contract quotas; loudest if misclassified)
                if "kind" not in p:
                    p["kind"] = "user-facing"
                for t in p.get("tasks", []):
                    # title ŌåÆ name
                    if "title" in t and "name" not in t:
                        t["name"] = t.pop("title")
                    # files ŌåÆ files_to_modify (conservative default)
                    if "files" in t and "files_to_modify" not in t and "files_to_create" not in t:
                        t["files_to_modify"] = t.pop("files")
            return cls.model_validate(raw)
        except json.JSONDecodeError as e:
            logger.error("PLAN.json is not valid JSON: %s", e)
            return None
        except Exception as e:
            logger.error("PLAN.json validation failed: %s", e)
            return None

    @staticmethod
    def json_template() -> str:
        """JSON template string for the plan creation prompt."""
        return """{
  "title": "Feature-Titel",
  "summary": "Kurze Beschreibung was implementiert wird",
  "size": "standard",
  "assumptions": [
    "Annahmen die du ├╝ber die Codebase/das Feature machst",
    "z.B. 'Tests laufen mit pytest', 'API ist REST-basiert'"
  ],
  "out_of_scope": [
    "Was NICHT Teil dieses Plans ist",
    "z.B. 'Deployment/CI-Pipeline', 'Performance-Optimierung'"
  ],
  "phases": [
    {
      "id": "01",
      "name": "Phase-Name",
      "kind": "user-facing|integration|infrastructure",
      "description": "Was in dieser Phase passiert",
      "merge_justification": "Ein Satz: warum diese Phase nicht mit der naechsten gemerged werden kann. Leer nur bei der letzten Phase.",
      "tasks": [
        {
          "id": "01-01",
          "name": "Task-Name",
          "description": "Detaillierte Beschreibung was zu tun ist",
          "files_to_create": ["liste/von/neuen/dateien.ext"],
          "files_to_modify": ["liste/von/existierenden/dateien.ext"],
          "depends_on": [],
          "model": "sonnet|haiku|opus (welches Modell f├╝r diesen Task am besten ist)",
          "effort": "low|medium|high (wie viel Denkaufwand n├Čtig ist)",
          "test_level": "L1|L2| (welches Test-Level nach diesem Task laufen soll, leer wenn kein Test n├Čtig)",
          "convention_skills": ["skill-name-1", "skill-name-2 (aus .opencode/skills/, leer wenn keine)"],
          "relevant_req_ids": ["R3", "R7 (IDs aus SPEC.compact.json ŌĆö welche Requirements dieser Task adressiert)"]
        }
      ]
    }
  ],
  "estimated_complexity": "low|medium|high",
  "risks": ["Liste von potentiellen Risiken"]
}"""

def validate(path: str):
    errors = []
    warnings = []

    with open(path) as f:
        data = json.load(f)

    if not isinstance(data, dict):
        errors.append("Root must be a JSON object")
        return errors, warnings

    # Required: phases
    if "phases" not in data:
        errors.append("Missing required field: 'phases'")
        return errors, warnings

    phases = data["phases"]
    if not isinstance(phases, list):
        errors.append("'phases' must be a list")
        return errors, warnings

    # Resolve size ŌåÆ caps
    size = data.get("size", "standard")
    if size not in VALID_SIZES:
        errors.append(f"'size' must be one of {sorted(VALID_SIZES)} (got '{size}')")
        size = "standard"  # fall through with default caps for the rest of validation
    max_phases = LARGE_MAX_PHASES if size == "large" else DEFAULT_MAX_PHASES
    max_tasks_per_phase = (
        LARGE_MAX_TASKS_PER_PHASE if size == "large" else DEFAULT_MAX_TASKS_PER_PHASE
    )

    if len(phases) < 1:
        errors.append("'phases' must have at least 1 item")
    if len(phases) > max_phases:
        hint = (
            " Or set 'size': 'large' to raise caps to 5├Ś5 if the feature genuinely doesn't fit."
            if size == "standard"
            else ""
        )
        errors.append(
            f"'phases' has {len(phases)} items; max is {max_phases} (size='{size}'). "
            f"If the feature genuinely needs more, split the SPEC into multiple runs.{hint}"
        )

content.text: Now add the "Quick route override" section between "When to use `size: large`" and "Rules", and tighten Rule 1.

input.old_string:
Max 1 infrastructure phase and "final phase ŌēĀ infrastructure" apply regardless
of size.

## Rules

1. **Max 4 phases ├Ś 4 tasks per phase** (default). Pydantic and the validator both reject violations. Set `"size": "large"` to raise caps to 5├Ś5 when genuinely needed ŌĆö otherwise split the SPEC into multiple dirigent runs.
input.new_string:
Max 1 infrastructure phase and "final phase ŌēĀ infrastructure" apply regardless
of size.

## Quick route override

When `${DIRIGENT_RUN_DIR}/route.json` reports `route: "quick"`, the plan shape
is constrained ŌĆö regardless of `size`:

- **Max 1 phase, max 6 tasks** in that phase.
- The single phase has no `merge_justification` (it's also the last phase).
- Pick `kind: "user-facing"` or `"integration"` ŌĆö never `"infrastructure"` (the
  "final phase ŌēĀ infrastructure" rule still holds, and quick has only one phase).
- Do not set `"size": "large"`. The route override wins; the validator enforces
  the 1├Ś6 cap regardless.

If the feature genuinely doesn't fit 1├Ś6, the route was misjudged ŌĆö surface that
to the user and recommend re-running with a different route (`hybrid` for feature
work on an existing repo, `greenfield` for new projects).

## Rules

1. **Plan caps depend on route and size.** Quick route: 1 phase ├Ś 6 tasks (overrides size). Standard size: 4 phases ├Ś 4 tasks. Large size: 5 phases ├Ś 5 tasks. Pydantic and the validator both reject violations. Set `"size": "large"` only on non-quick routes when 4├Ś4 genuinely doesn't fit ŌĆö otherwise split the SPEC into multiple dirigent runs.

1	"""
2	Unit tests for plan_schema.py ŌĆö Plan, Phase, Task models.
3	
4	Covers: properties, save/load roundtrip, backward compatibility normalization.
5	"""
6	
7	import json
8	
9	
10	from outbid_dirigent.plan_schema import Phase, Plan, Task
11	
12	
13	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
14	# Helper
15	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
16	
17	
18	def _simple_plan() -> Plan:
19	    return Plan(
20	        title="Test Feature",
21	        phases=[
22	            Phase(
23	                id="01",
24	                name="Core",
25	                tasks=[
26	                    Task(id="01-01", name="Setup DB"),
27	                    Task(id="01-02", name="Add API"),
28	                ],
29	            ),
30	            Phase(
31	                id="02",
32	                name="Ship",
33	                tasks=[
34	                    Task(id="02-01", name="Create PR"),
35	                ],
36	            ),
37	        ],
38	    )
39	
40	
41	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
42	# TestPlanProperties
43	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
44	
45	
46	class TestPlanProperties:
47	    def test_total_tasks(self):
48	        plan = _simple_plan()
49	        assert plan.total_tasks == 3
50	
51	    def test_total_tasks_empty(self):
52	        plan = Plan()
53	        assert plan.total_tasks == 0
54	
55	    def test_all_tasks_flat_list(self):
56	        plan = _simple_plan()
57	        result = plan.all_tasks
58	        assert len(result) == 3
59	        # Each entry is a (Task, Phase) tuple
60	        task_ids = [t.id for t, _ in result]
61	        assert task_ids == ["01-01", "01-02", "02-01"]
62	
63	    def test_all_tasks_correct_phases(self):
64	        plan = _simple_plan()
65	        result = plan.all_tasks
66	        phase_ids = [p.id for _, p in result]
67	        assert phase_ids == ["01", "01", "02"]
68	
69	
70	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
71	# TestTaskPosition
72	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
73	
74	
75	class TestTaskPosition:
76	    def test_first_task_no_prev_has_next(self):
77	        plan = _simple_plan()
78	        pos = plan.task_position("01-01")
79	        assert pos is not None
80	        assert pos["index"] == 1
81	        assert pos["total"] == 3
82	        assert pos["phase_id"] == "01"
83	        assert pos["phase_name"] == "Core"
84	        assert pos["total_phases"] == 2
85	        assert "prev_id" not in pos
86	        assert "prev_name" not in pos
87	        assert pos["next_id"] == "01-02"
88	        assert pos["next_name"] == "Add API"
89	
90	    def test_middle_task_has_prev_and_next(self):
91	        plan = _simple_plan()
92	        pos = plan.task_position("01-02")
93	        assert pos is not None
94	        assert pos["index"] == 2
95	        assert pos["prev_id"] == "01-01"
96	        assert pos["prev_name"] == "Setup DB"
97	        assert pos["next_id"] == "02-01"
98	        assert pos["next_name"] == "Create PR"
99	
100	    def test_last_task_has_prev_no_next(self):
101	        plan = _simple_plan()
102	        pos = plan.task_position("02-01")
103	        assert pos is not None
104	        assert pos["index"] == 3
105	        assert pos["total"] == 3
106	        assert pos["phase_id"] == "02"
107	        assert pos["phase_name"] == "Ship"
108	        assert pos["prev_id"] == "01-02"
109	        assert pos["prev_name"] == "Add API"
110	        assert "next_id" not in pos
111	        assert "next_name" not in pos
112	
113	    def test_nonexistent_task_returns_none(self):
114	        plan = _simple_plan()
115	        assert plan.task_position("does-not-exist") is None
116	
117	
118	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
119	# TestPlanSaveLoad
120	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
121	
122	
123	class TestPlanSaveLoad:
124	    def test_roundtrip(self, tmp_path):
125	        plan = _simple_plan()
126	        dest = tmp_path / "PLAN.json"
127	        plan.save(dest)
128	        loaded = Plan.load(dest)
129	        assert loaded is not None
130	        assert loaded.title == "Test Feature"
131	        assert loaded.total_tasks == 3
132	        assert loaded.phases[0].id == "01"
133	        assert loaded.phases[1].id == "02"
134	
135	    def test_missing_file_returns_none(self, tmp_path):
136	        result = Plan.load(tmp_path / "nonexistent.json")
137	        assert result is None
138	
139	    def test_invalid_json_returns_none(self, tmp_path):
140	        bad = tmp_path / "bad.json"
141	        bad.write_text("{ this is not valid json }", encoding="utf-8")
142	        assert Plan.load(bad) is None
143	
144	    def test_backward_compat_phase_key_renamed_to_id(self, tmp_path):
145	        """Old format uses 'phase' instead of 'id' for phase identifier."""
146	        raw = {
147	            "title": "Legacy Plan",
148	            "phases": [
149	                {
150	                    "phase": "01",
151	                    "name": "Old Phase",
152	                    "tasks": [],
153	                }
154	            ],

"""
Unit tests for plan_schema.py ŌĆö Plan, Phase, Task models.

Covers: properties, save/load roundtrip, backward compatibility normalization.
"""

import json


from outbid_dirigent.plan_schema import Phase, Plan, Task


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Helper
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------


def _simple_plan() -> Plan:
    return Plan(
        title="Test Feature",
        phases=[
            Phase(
                id="01",
                name="Core",
                tasks=[
                    Task(id="01-01", name="Setup DB"),
                    Task(id="01-02", name="Add API"),
                ],
            ),
            Phase(
                id="02",
                name="Ship",
                tasks=[
                    Task(id="02-01", name="Create PR"),
                ],
            ),
        ],
    )


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# TestPlanProperties
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------


class TestPlanProperties:
    def test_total_tasks(self):
        plan = _simple_plan()
        assert plan.total_tasks == 3

    def test_total_tasks_empty(self):
        plan = Plan()
        assert plan.total_tasks == 0

    def test_all_tasks_flat_list(self):
        plan = _simple_plan()
        result = plan.all_tasks
        assert len(result) == 3
        # Each entry is a (Task, Phase) tuple
        task_ids = [t.id for t, _ in result]
        assert task_ids == ["01-01", "01-02", "02-01"]

    def test_all_tasks_correct_phases(self):
        plan = _simple_plan()
        result = plan.all_tasks
        phase_ids = [p.id for _, p in result]
        assert phase_ids == ["01", "01", "02"]


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# TestTaskPosition
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------


class TestTaskPosition:
    def test_first_task_no_prev_has_next(self):
        plan = _simple_plan()
        pos = plan.task_position("01-01")
        assert pos is not None
        assert pos["index"] == 1
        assert pos["total"] == 3
        assert pos["phase_id"] == "01"
        assert pos["phase_name"] == "Core"
        assert pos["total_phases"] == 2
        assert "prev_id" not in pos
        assert "prev_name" not in pos
        assert pos["next_id"] == "01-02"
        assert pos["next_name"] == "Add API"

    def test_middle_task_has_prev_and_next(self):
        plan = _simple_plan()
        pos = plan.task_position("01-02")
        assert pos is not None
        assert pos["index"] == 2
        assert pos["prev_id"] == "01-01"
        assert pos["prev_name"] == "Setup DB"
        assert pos["next_id"] == "02-01"
        assert pos["next_name"] == "Create PR"

    def test_last_task_has_prev_no_next(self):
        plan = _simple_plan()
        pos = plan.task_position("02-01")
        assert pos is not None
        assert pos["index"] == 3
        assert pos["total"] == 3
        assert pos["phase_id"] == "02"
        assert pos["phase_name"] == "Ship"
        assert pos["prev_id"] == "01-02"
        assert pos["prev_name"] == "Add API"
        assert "next_id" not in pos
        assert "next_name" not in pos

    def test_nonexistent_task_returns_none(self):
        plan = _simple_plan()
        assert plan.task_position("does-not-exist") is None


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# TestPlanSaveLoad
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------


class TestPlanSaveLoad:
    def test_roundtrip(self, tmp_path):
        plan = _simple_plan()
        dest = tmp_path / "PLAN.json"
        plan.save(dest)
        loaded = Plan.load(dest)
        assert loaded is not None
        assert loaded.title == "Test Feature"
        assert loaded.total_tasks == 3
        assert loaded.phases[0].id == "01"
        assert loaded.phases[1].id == "02"

    def test_missing_file_returns_none(self, tmp_path):
        result = Plan.load(tmp_path / "nonexistent.json")
        assert result is None

    def test_invalid_json_returns_none(self, tmp_path):
        bad = tmp_path / "bad.json"
        bad.write_text("{ this is not valid json }", encoding="utf-8")
        assert Plan.load(bad) is None

    def test_backward_compat_phase_key_renamed_to_id(self, tmp_path):
        """Old format uses 'phase' instead of 'id' for phase identifier."""
        raw = {
            "title": "Legacy Plan",
            "phases": [
                {
                    "phase": "01",
                    "name": "Old Phase",
                    "tasks": [],
                }
            ],
        }
        dest = tmp_path / "old.json"
        dest.write_text(json.dumps(raw), encoding="utf-8")
        loaded = Plan.load(dest)
        assert loaded is not None
        assert loaded.phases[0].id == "01"
        assert loaded.phases[0].name == "Old Phase"

    def test_numeric_phase_id_stringified(self, tmp_path):
        """Numeric phase ids (e.g. 1) must be stringified to '1'."""
        raw = {
            "title": "Numeric IDs",
            "phases": [
                {
                    "id": 1,
                    "name": "First Phase",
                    "tasks": [],
                },
                {
                    "id": 2,
                    "name": "Second Phase",
                    "tasks": [],
                },
            ],
        }
        dest = tmp_path / "numeric.json"
        dest.write_text(json.dumps(raw), encoding="utf-8")
        loaded = Plan.load(dest)
        assert loaded is not None
        assert loaded.phases[0].id == "1"
        assert loaded.phases[1].id == "2"

"""
Unit tests for the create-plan validator script.

Covers:
- Standard caps (4x4) when no route.json is present
- Large caps (5x5) when size="large"
- Quick route override (1x6) regardless of size
- Pydantic-allowed shapes still validate at the script layer

The script lives outside the importable package, so we load it via importlib
from its absolute path.
"""

from __future__ import annotations

import importlib.util
import json
import sys
from pathlib import Path
from types import ModuleType


SCRIPT_PATH = (
    Path(__file__).resolve().parents[1]
    / "src/outbid_dirigent/plugin/skills/create-plan/scripts/validate_schema.py"
)


def _load_validator() -> ModuleType:
    spec = importlib.util.spec_from_file_location("validate_schema", SCRIPT_PATH)
    assert spec is not None and spec.loader is not None
    module = importlib.util.module_from_spec(spec)
    sys.modules["validate_schema"] = module
    spec.loader.exec_module(module)
    return module


validator = _load_validator()


def _make_phase(idx: int, n_tasks: int, *, kind: str = "user-facing", last: bool = False) -> dict:
    pid = f"{idx:02d}"
    return {
        "id": pid,
        "name": f"Phase {pid}",
        "kind": kind,
        "merge_justification": "" if last else "Splitting keeps the slice reviewable.",
        "tasks": [
            {"id": f"{pid}-{j:02d}", "name": f"Ship slice {pid}-{j:02d}"}
            for j in range(1, n_tasks + 1)
        ],
    }


def _make_plan(phases: list[dict], *, size: str = "standard") -> dict:
    return {
        "title": "Test Plan",
        "summary": "test",
        "size": size,
        "phases": phases,
    }


def _write(tmp_path: Path, plan: dict, route: str | None = None) -> Path:
    plan_path = tmp_path / "PLAN.json"
    plan_path.write_text(json.dumps(plan), encoding="utf-8")
    if route is not None:
        (tmp_path / "route.json").write_text(json.dumps({"route": route}), encoding="utf-8")
    return plan_path


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Standard route (no route.json) ŌĆö caps unchanged
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------


class TestStandardCapsRegression:
    def test_4_phases_4_tasks_passes(self, tmp_path):
        phases = [_make_phase(i, 4) for i in range(1, 5)]
        phases[-1]["merge_justification"] = ""
        plan_path = _write(tmp_path, _make_plan(phases))
        errors, _ = validator.validate(str(plan_path))
        assert errors == [], f"Expected no errors, got: {errors}"

    def test_5_phases_rejected_at_standard(self, tmp_path):
        phases = [_make_phase(i, 1) for i in range(1, 6)]
        phases[-1]["merge_justification"] = ""
        plan_path = _write(tmp_path, _make_plan(phases))
        errors, _ = validator.validate(str(plan_path))
        assert any("max is 4" in e and "size='standard'" in e for e in errors)

    def test_large_size_allows_5_phases(self, tmp_path):
        phases = [_make_phase(i, 1) for i in range(1, 6)]
        phases[-1]["merge_justification"] = ""
        plan_path = _write(tmp_path, _make_plan(phases, size="large"))
        errors, _ = validator.validate(str(plan_path))
        assert errors == [], f"Expected no errors, got: {errors}"


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Quick route override
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------


class TestQuickRouteCaps:
    def test_quick_one_phase_six_tasks_passes(self, tmp_path):
        phases = [_make_phase(1, 6, last=True)]
        plan_path = _write(tmp_path, _make_plan(phases), route="quick")
        errors, _ = validator.validate(str(plan_path))
        assert errors == [], f"Expected no errors, got: {errors}"

    def test_quick_two_phases_rejected(self, tmp_path):
        phases = [_make_phase(1, 1), _make_phase(2, 1, last=True)]
        plan_path = _write(tmp_path, _make_plan(phases), route="quick")
        errors, _ = validator.validate(str(plan_path))
        assert any(
            "max is 1" in e and "route='quick'" in e for e in errors
        ), f"Expected quick-cap rejection, got: {errors}"

    def test_quick_seven_tasks_rejected(self, tmp_path):
        phases = [_make_phase(1, 7, last=True)]
        plan_path = _write(tmp_path, _make_plan(phases), route="quick")
        errors, _ = validator.validate(str(plan_path))
        # Note: Pydantic itself caps tasks at 6 ŌĆö but the validator script does its
        # own counting, so it sees the raw JSON (7 tasks) and rejects with the
        # route-aware message.
        assert any(
            "max is 6" in e and "route='quick'" in e for e in errors
        ), f"Expected per-phase task-cap rejection, got: {errors}"

    def test_quick_size_large_does_not_lift_caps(self, tmp_path):
        """Route override wins: quick + size=large still capped at 1x6."""
        phases = [_make_phase(1, 1), _make_phase(2, 1, last=True)]
        plan_path = _write(tmp_path, _make_plan(phases, size="large"), route="quick")
        errors, _ = validator.validate(str(plan_path))
        assert any(
            "max is 1" in e and "route='quick'" in e for e in errors
        ), f"Expected quick caps to win over size=large, got: {errors}"

    def test_non_quick_route_uses_size_caps(self, tmp_path):
        """A hybrid-route plan with 4 phases ├Ś 4 tasks still passes under standard caps."""
        phases = [_make_phase(i, 4) for i in range(1, 5)]
        phases[-1]["merge_justification"] = ""
        plan_path = _write(tmp_path, _make_plan(phases), route="hybrid")
        errors, _ = validator.validate(str(plan_path))
        assert errors == [], f"Expected no errors, got: {errors}"

    def test_corrupt_route_json_falls_back_to_size(self, tmp_path):
        """Garbage in route.json must not crash the validator; fall back to size caps."""
        phases = [_make_phase(i, 4) for i in range(1, 5)]
        phases[-1]["merge_justification"] = ""
        plan_path = _write(tmp_path, _make_plan(phases))
        (tmp_path / "route.json").write_text("not json at all", encoding="utf-8")
        errors, _ = validator.validate(str(plan_path))
        assert errors == [], f"Expected fallback to standard caps, got: {errors}"

input.command:
git -C /Users/jk/projects/outbid-dirigent commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
feat(quick-route): cap plans at 1 phase ├Ś 6 tasks

Make the create-plan skill route-aware: when route.json reports
route="quick", the validator enforces a 1-phase ├Ś 6-task cap instead of
the standard 4├Ś4 (or 5├Ś5 for size="large"). The route override wins over
size, so quick-route plans cannot be widened by setting size="large".

Why: --route quick was previously identical to other routes during
EXECUTION because the LLM-produced plan could come back as 4 phases ├Ś 4
tasks regardless of route. Capping the plan shape is the cheapest fix
that preserves the existing executor (phase contracts and per-phase
review still run, intentionally ŌĆö they're cheap safety nets even for a
single-phase plan).

Changes:
- plan_schema.py: bump Phase.tasks max_length 5 ŌåÆ 6 to allow the new
  quick-route cap. Standard/large routes are still bounded by the
  validator at 4 / 5.
- create-plan/scripts/validate_schema.py: read route.json next to
  PLAN.json and apply QUICK_MAX_PHASES=1, QUICK_MAX_TASKS_PER_PHASE=6
  when route=="quick". Falls back gracefully on missing/corrupt
  route.json.
- create-plan/SKILL.md: list route.json as an input and add a "Quick
  route override" section documenting the constraint.
- tests/test_validate_schema.py: 9 new cases covering quick-route
  acceptance/rejection, size-large override behavior, and standard-route
  regressions.

This change is significantly AI-assisted (Claude Opus 4.7).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
)"
